title: Fixing the Node.js Express Middleware Route Redirect

statement.markdown: Write a Node.js Express middleware function that handles HTTP GET requests to the root URL and redirects the client to a different URL. However, the implementation contains a subtle bug that will cause unexpected behavior. Fix the bug to ensure the redirect works correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The input is a JSON object containing the redirect URL.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a HTTP 302 redirect response.
statement.notes: The redirect should only happen on HTTP GET requests.

constraints.description: The number of redirect requests is capped at 10000

examples.explanation: This example demonstrates a successful redirect on HTTP GET request.

examples.output: HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request Invalid redirect URL
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates an error handling case where the redirect URL is invalid.
This example demonstrates a successful redirect on HTTP GET request.

hints.text: Check the HTTP request method before redirecting
Verify the redirect URL is valid before proceeding

editorial.title: Fixing the Node.js Express Middleware Route Redirect
editorial.intuition: The correct approach involves checking the HTTP request method and verifying the redirect URL before redirecting the client.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute force approach is to use a try-catch block to catch any exceptions that occur during the redirect process.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution involves using a more elegant approach to handle the HTTP request method and redirect URL validation.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution utilizes a middleware function to check the HTTP request method and redirect URL before redirecting the client. It also includes error handling for invalid redirect URLs.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The solution is correct because it checks the HTTP request method and verifies the redirect URL before redirecting the client. It also includes error handling for invalid redirect URLs.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: 1. Check the HTTP request method. 2. Verify the redirect URL is valid. 3. Redirect the client if the HTTP request method is GET and the redirect URL is valid.
commonMistakes: Failing to check the HTTP request method

testCases.output: HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request Invalid redirect URL
